sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me understand the maze structure better. The player (blue 4x4 square, node 6) moved right from cols 14-18 to cols 20-24. The maze seems to have:
- Black (B) walls
- White (W) corridors/chambers
- Red (R) path on the right side
- Blue (b) player and target areas
Looking at the layout, the player is currently at rows 8-12, cols 20-24 (inside the black maze). The white corridors are at different positions. The red path goes from rows 9-13 on the right, then down to row 42.
I need to understand the movement mechanics better. The player moved 6 columns right. Let me try DOWN to see where it goes, and also understand the maze connectivity.
Actually, let me first try to LEFT to go back and verify the movement is reversible, then try DOWN.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: The player is the blue 4x4 square (node 6) inside the black maze. It moved right by 6 columns when RIGHT was pressed. The maze has white corridors and a red path. The goal appears to be reaching the blue square at bottom-right (node 11).
Action model: Directional keys move the player within the maze. Movement jumps by 6 cells (one "room" width).
Plan: Explore the maze by trying DOWN to understand connectivity, then map out the path to the goal.
